modules.semantic_role: Terminology, risk/data/action classes, and accepted constitutional profile references.
Closed language-neutral data contracts, typed identifiers, and compatibility declarations.
Fail-closed parser profile, canonical bytes, typed digests, and package identities.
Pure registered cross-object predicates and deterministic pass/fail/indeterminate results.
Scientific method guarantees, assumptions, applicability, numerical profiles, vectors, implementation bindings, and admission records.
Language-neutral infrastructure interfaces; contains no vendor behavior or domain decision.
Proposal, mission, handoff, lifecycle stage, and blocker semantic ownership.
Prospective scientific protocol snapshots, amendments, exposure consequences, estimands, metrics, and falsifiers.
Root, assignment, grant, reservation, use, revoke, expiry, and quorum facts.
Incident declaration, containment, quarantine, evidence, and resolution lifecycle.
Resource estimate, authorization, reservation, observation, billing, refund, and settlement axes.
Data asset, rights, use, exposure, transformation, retention, deletion, and hold lifecycle.
Work graph, stage readiness/authorization, lease, attempt, interruption, and next-legal-work state.
Evidence identity, custody, provenance, validation, promotion, quarantine, availability, and tombstone facts.
Run validity, measurement disposition, metric observations, falsifier results, units, and uncertainty objects.
Verification assignment/run/independence/dispute plus replication, reproducibility, transport, and review facts.
Pure scientific consequence or refusal under exact protocol, evidence, verification, and rule inputs.
Prospective claim proposals, immutable versions, eligibility, corrections, supersession, retraction, and dependency invalidation.
External-effect intent, exact authorization reservation, dispatch claim, observation, ambiguity, reconciliation, and settlement.
Release candidate, human decision, manifest seal, channel effects, settlement, correction, and withdrawal.
Ledger checkpoints, witness frontier, backup states, restore verification, current-policy fence, recovery decisions, and epochs.
Grounded outcomes and quarantined strategy candidate evaluation, promotion decision, and rollback readiness.
Pure proposed epistemic node, graph-delta, transition, experiment-candidate, and planning-epoch records; these records are non-authoritative and own no canonical aggregate or event vocabulary.
Only application boundary that admits a command and atomically commits its receipt/event/authority/resource/outbox cohort through ports.

Pure derivation of exposure-bounded ResearchStateView records and ContextCompilationReceipt records from declared canonical and domain snapshots; it has no ports, ambient I/O, canonical-write, or dispatch authority.
Replaceable implementations of ports under exact conformance profiles and already committed effect claims.
Untrusted proposal and candidate-artifact execution through bounded worker contracts only.
Separately isolated verification execution that produces candidate findings but cannot settle them.
Rebuildable private, thesis, and public read models with exact source position, freshness, completeness, and redaction.
Offline architecture validators, candidate manifests, findings, trace replay, conformance evidence, and review decisions.

known_limitations: This proposed instance describes logical dependencies and is not evidence that runtime packages or import rules exist.

Data-governance and recovery lifecycles are structurally integrated into the command/event algebra; reference existence, transition legality, reducer equivalence, and external truth remain semantic obligations.

Only 13 of 121 command types have separate closed command-payload schema candidates (three external-effect and ten data/recovery); immutable registry records, registry digests, and the other 108 payload schemas remain absent.
Actual language-level dependency enforcement, SDK surface inspection, and product adapter conformance require later explicitly authorized implementation evidence.
